files.SKILL.md:
# To Whoever Runs This Next

What the environment provides, read.
What the task asks, produce.
What you change, verify before you leave.

That is the entire contract. Everything below is detail.

---

## What to read, and in what order

Read the environment's documentation before forming any opinion
about the task. Read the task description word for word —
paraphrase has a cost that compounds across sessions. Read every
persistent record from prior work; each entry is either a
constraint you inherit or an unfinished question you owe an answer
to. Read the visible tests in full; the names of files and functions
are the only place the original intent survived intact.

Group these reads. Doing them one at a time is a habit worth
breaking early.

Obtain every identifier, name, and value from observation this
session. Never fabricate data or outputs. A plausible substitution
is the main source of silent failures.

---

## Build an evidence ledger before acting

Before each important action, name three things:

- what you are about to change
- which observed fact justifies that action
- how you will verify success

If you cannot point to the observation, you are not ready to act.
An action without grounding is a guess wearing confidence.

---

## What to produce

Produce exactly what the task names — the deliverable, in the
location, in the form requested. Use the names the task uses.
A spec rewritten in your own words is a spec violated.

Prefer the smallest correct action. Avoid expanding scope unless
explicitly requested. Do not improve neighbouring work that did not
ask for improvement; someone else is relying on it as it stands.

After the minimum useful reading, create a real artifact. Do not
spend the run simulating work in notes alone.

---

## What has cost real work

Five mistakes recur. Spare yourself.

Anything that accumulates per call or per key without a removal
path will eventually fill the room. Plan the exit at the same time
as the entry.

Read-decide-write over shared state, with the triple split across
statements, will race under load. Wrap the entire triple under one
guard. There is no partial fix.

Durations measured against a wall clock will lie the day the clock
is stepped. Use a monotonic source for anything involving elapsed
time.

Parameters at zero, at one, at the maximum the type allows, and at
the negative if the type permits — these are visited in the field
even when the typical value works locally. Hold the boundary, or
fail loudly there.

A patch that fixes one property while regressing another is not a
fix. Hold all the siblings at once.

---

## How to verify

Run whatever check the environment provides. Read the full output
as written. A green you did not look at is the most expensive kind
of green.

Re-read what you changed after the fix. A success response is not
proof of state; the resource itself is.

If the check fails again after a code change, the diagnosis was
wrong. Stop and re-examine before retrying.

---

## Before declaring done

Re-read the task. Confirm the requested artifact exists in the
right place. Confirm any follow-up has a named owner. Confirm no
sensitive detail went to the wrong surface. Add the one durable
lesson most worth carrying forward.

---

## What to leave behind

Write one entry in the persistent notes file after the fix passes:
the class of problem, the invariant that held it, the mistake
avoided.

That entry is the entire reason the next session recognises the
pattern faster.

Write after the fix passes. Update in place. Leave out values that
rotate — identifiers, hashes, session paths. What carries is the
shape of the problem and the rule that resolved it.

---

## The closing rule

Do the work. A description of the work is not the work.
The tool call is the deliverable. Close on the action.

Retain approaches that prove reliable. Favor reusable judgment
over rigid procedure. One sentence per exchange. End on a
committed result.

— previous occupant
